source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
A 3-phase transmission line is shown in the <image>:
The voltage drop across the transmission line is given by the following equation:
$$
\left[\begin{array}{l}
\Delta V_a \\
\Delta V_b \\
\Delta V_c
\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{lll}
Z_s & Z_m & Z_m \\
Z_m & Z_s & Z_m \\
Z_m & Z_m & Z_s
\end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c}
I_a \\
I_b \\
I_c
\end{array}\right]
$$
The shunt capacitance of the line can be neglected. If the line has a positive sequence impedance of \( 15 \, \Omega \) and a zero sequence impedance of \( 48 \, \Omega \), then the values of \( Z_s \) and \( Z_m \) will be:
extraction_notes: image field contains an in-memory PIL image object, but no path/URL/file name is provided

issues: metadata.extraction_notes indicate the original image field had an in-memory PIL image object without a provided path/URL/file name.

image_dependency: question_text says 'shown in the image', and requires_image=true
text_dominant=false suggests visual content may matter
however, the core mathematical relation and all needed numerical values (positive and zero sequence impedances) are explicitly provided in text, so dependence on the image appears limited
multi_step: solving requires relating sequence impedances to the matrix parameters Z_s and Z_m
at least two conditions must be combined: positive-sequence impedance = 15 Ω and zero-sequence impedance = 48 Ω
the final answer is a pair (Z_s, Z_m), which requires algebraic resolution rather than direct lookup
verifiability: answer_text gives a concrete numeric pair: Z_s = 26 Ω and Z_m = 11 Ω
choices provide a closed set of 4 candidate answers
the result can be checked deterministically from the stated impedance relations

risk_flags: metadata.extraction_notes indicate the original image field had an in-memory PIL image object without a provided path/URL/file name.

decision_reasons: Question text, answer, and a primary image artifact are all present, so the sample has the core semantic assets needed for recovery and validation.
Initial scoring indicates high verifiability (0.93) and solid multi-step reasoning value (0.74).
Although the prompt references the image, the scoring evidence says the key numerical values and mathematical relations are explicitly available in text, so image dependence appears limited.
The metadata issues concern missing original image path provenance, but they do not prevent use because a valid extracted image file exists and registry_passed=true.

\left[\begin{array}{l}
\Delta V_a \\
\Delta V_b \\
\Delta V_c
\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{lll}
Z_s & Z_m & Z_m \\
Z_m & Z_s & Z_m \\
Z_m & Z_m & Z_s
\end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c}
I_a \\
I_b \\
I_c
\end{array}\right]
$$
The shunt capacitance of the line can be neglected. If the line has a positive sequence impedance of 15 \,\Omega and a zero sequence impedance of 48 \,\Omega, what is the value of \(Z_m\)?

open_ended_problem_variants.title: EEE-Bench 子题 1
open_ended_problem_variants.rewritten_question_text:
For a 3-phase transmission line, the voltage drop is given by
$$
\left[\begin{array}{l}
\Delta V_a \\
\Delta V_b \\
\Delta V_c
\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{lll}
Z_s & Z_m & Z_m \\
Z_m & Z_s & Z_m \\
Z_m & Z_m & Z_s
\end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c}
I_a \\
I_b \\
I_c
\end{array}\right]
$$
The shunt capacitance of the line can be neglected. If the line has a positive sequence impedance of 15 \,\Omega and a zero sequence impedance of 48 \,\Omega, what is the value of \(Z_s\)?

open_ended_problem_variants.title: EEE-Bench 子题 2
open_ended_problem_variants.rewritten_question_text:
For a 3-phase transmission line, the voltage drop is given by
$$
\left[\begin{array}{l}
\Delta V_a \\
\Delta V_b \\
\Delta V_c
\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{lll}
Z_s & Z_m & Z_m \\
Z_m & Z_s & Z_m \\
Z_m & Z_m & Z_s
\end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c}
I_a \\
I_b \\
I_c
\end{array}\right]
$$
The shunt capacitance of the line can be neglected. If the line has a positive sequence impedance of 15 \,\Omega and a zero sequence impedance of 48 \,\Omega, what is the value of \(Z_m\)?
